title: Pyrus humilis
intro: Pyrus humilis, also known as the dwarf pear or mountain pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The health benefits of Pyrus humilis include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for cardiovascular and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus humilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus humilis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments in Central Asia.
taxonomy: Pyrus humilis is classified within the family Rosaceae, which includes a wide range of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. This family encompasses over 2,000 species of plants, including roses, apples, pears, and plums. Pyrus humilis, being a member of the Rosaceae family, is closely related to other species within this group.
